narrative:
Sidny Lopes Cabral is a right-back with an impressive overall rating of 7.13/10. This places him among the top 3% of right-backs at this World Cup. His rating is weighted 67% from club form and 33% from this World Cup.

Cabral demonstrates a clinical touch, scoring 0.315 goals per 90 minutes at this World Cup, significantly outperforming his 0.032 expected goals. Defensively, he is active, making 4.72 clearances and 1.573 tackles per 90 minutes. However, his 0.031 expected assists per 90 minutes indicate a limited direct creative output.

Cabral appears to be a defensively solid right-back who contributes unexpectedly with goals for his national team. Scouts and fans should continue to watch his exceptional goal-scoring efficiency, given his low expected goals. His robust defensive output, particularly his clearances, will also be key in upcoming matches.
